rawText:
To translate a literary text is not merely to transpose words from one language into another but to negotiate an impossible set of competing demands — fidelity to the source text's meaning, attention to its rhythm and register, sensitivity to the target language's own expressive resources — each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others.

The translator confronts, at every sentence, the foundational problem that languages do not map onto one another with anything approaching one-to-one correspondence: a single word in the source language may carry connotations, etymological echoes, and cultural associations for which the target language offers no ready equivalent, forcing the translator to choose which dimension of meaning to preserve and which to sacrifice.

This is why the debate between domestication and foreignization — whether the translation should read as though the author had originally written in the target language, or whether it should retain the strangeness of the source — is not a technical dispute but a philosophical one, reflecting fundamentally different assumptions about what a translation is for.

The domesticating translator privileges accessibility, smoothing away the linguistic and cultural peculiarities of the original so that the reader encounters a text that feels native; the foreignizing translator, by contrast, insists on preserving those very peculiarities, accepting that the reader will sometimes stumble over unfamiliar constructions, because it is precisely in that stumbling that the reader encounters the otherness of the source culture.

What is lost in even the finest translation is not merely a list of untranslatable words but an entire ecology of sound and sense: the phonetic texture of the original prose, the rhythmic patterns that govern the reader's breath, the syntactic structures that distribute emphasis in ways the target language cannot replicate without distortion.

Consider the challenge of translating a novel whose meaning depends partly on register shifts — passages where the narrator's language moves between formal and colloquial, literary and vernacular — and recognize that in many target languages, the register system is organized along entirely different social and grammatical axes, so that what reads as a subtle tonal shift in the original may become either imperceptible or grotesquely exaggerated in translation.

Yet to describe translation as loss is to tell only half the story, for every competent translation also creates something the original lacks: it provides a new phonetic body for the text, generates fresh rhythmic possibilities unavailable in the source language, and occasionally discovers resonances and ambiguities that the original, constrained by its own linguistic system, could not have produced.

The notion of literary fidelity, therefore, is far more complex than simple accuracy of denotation; it encompasses a translator's intuition about which qualities of the source text are most essential — its tone, its pace, its emotional architecture — and a willingness to depart from literal meaning when literal meaning would betray the spirit of the whole.

The best translations, like the best critical interpretations, do not pretend to be transparent windows onto the original but acknowledge themselves as readings — partial, situated, and inevitably shaped by the translator's own literary sensibility, cultural position, and historical moment.

What translation finally reveals is not the inadequacy of language but its inexhaustible generativity: every text, re-entered through a new linguistic system, yields possibilities that its author could not have foreseen, reminding us that meaning is not a fixed property of words but an emergent phenomenon, perpetually renewed by every act of reading across linguistic and cultural borders.

sentences.text: Translating a story is much harder than just changing words from one language to another.
sentences.translation: 이야기를 번역하는 것은 한 언어에서 다른 언어로 단어를 바꾸는 것보다 훨씬 어려워요.

sentences.text: Some words carry special feelings and ideas that do not exist in other languages.
sentences.translation: 어떤 단어들은 다른 언어에 존재하지 않는 특별한 느낌과 아이디어를 지니고 있어요.

sentences.text: Translators have to decide whether to make the story sound natural or keep it foreign.
sentences.translation: 번역자들은 이야기를 자연스럽게 들리게 할지 외국스러움을 유지할지 결정해야 해요.

sentences.text: Even the sound and rhythm of words get lost when a story moves to a new language.
sentences.translation: 이야기가 새 언어로 옮겨질 때 단어의 소리와 리듬까지 잃어버려요.

sentences.text: But translation can also add new beauty that the original story did not have.
sentences.translation: 하지만 번역은 원래 이야기에 없던 새로운 아름다움을 더할 수도 있어요.

sentences.text: Translation shows us that meaning is not fixed — it keeps growing with every new reading.
sentences.translation: 번역은 의미가 고정되어 있지 않다는 것을 보여줘요 — 새로 읽을 때마다 계속 자라나요.

sentences.text: Literary translation involves negotiating impossible trade-offs between fidelity to meaning, rhythm, register, and cultural context.
sentences.translation: 문학 번역은 의미, 리듬, 어조, 문화적 맥락에 대한 충실함 사이의 불가능한 절충을 협상하는 것을 포함해요.

sentences.text: The domestication vs. foreignization debate reflects philosophical disagreements about what translation should accomplish.
sentences.translation: 자국화 대 이국화 논쟁은 번역이 무엇을 달성해야 하는지에 대한 철학적 의견 불일치를 반영해요.

sentences.text: What is lost in translation includes not just words but an entire ecology of sound, rhythm, and syntax.
sentences.translation: 번역에서 잃어버리는 것은 단순히 단어가 아니라 소리, 리듬, 구문의 전체 생태계를 포함해요.

sentences.text: Register shifts that work subtly in the source language may become either invisible or exaggerated in translation.
sentences.translation: 원어에서 미묘하게 작동하는 어조 전환은 번역에서 보이지 않거나 과장될 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Literary fidelity goes beyond denotation to encompass tone, pace, and emotional architecture.
sentences.translation: 문학적 충실함은 지시적 의미를 넘어 어조, 속도, 정서적 구조를 포괄해요.

sentences.text: Translation reveals that meaning is not fixed but an emergent phenomenon renewed by every cross-cultural act of reading.
sentences.translation: 번역은 의미가 고정된 것이 아니라 모든 문화 간 읽기 행위에 의해 갱신되는 창발적 현상임을 드러내요.

sentences.text: This essay theorizes literary translation as the negotiation of incommensurable demands — semantic fidelity, prosodic equivalence, and cultural transposition — arguing that every choice privileges one dimension at the expense of others.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 문학 번역을 의미적 충실함, 운율적 등가, 문화적 전환이라는 공약 불가능한 요구의 협상으로 이론화하며, 모든 선택이 다른 차원을 희생하며 하나의 차원을 특권화한다고 논증해요.

sentences.text: The domestication/foreignization binary is reframed as a philosophical question about the ontology of translation itself — whether translation is assimilation or encounter.
sentences.translation: 자국화/이국화 이항대립은 번역 자체의 존재론에 대한 철학적 질문으로 재구성돼요 — 번역이 동화인지 만남인지의 문제예요.

sentences.text: The essay identifies what is lost as an "ecology of sound and sense" — a systemic concept that resists itemization and includes phonetic texture, rhythmic patterning, and syntactic distribution of emphasis.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 상실되는 것을 "소리와 의미의 생태계"로 식별해요 — 항목화에 저항하는 체계적 개념으로 음성적 질감, 리듬적 패턴화, 강조의 구문적 분배를 포함해요.

sentences.text: Register-shift translation is analyzed as a problem of incommensurable sociolinguistic systems, where the axes of formality differ structurally between source and target.
sentences.translation: 어조 전환 번역은 공약 불가능한 사회언어학적 체계의 문제로 분석되며, 격식성의 축이 원어와 목표어 사이에서 구조적으로 다른 경우를 다뤄요.

sentences.text: Translation is dialectically reframed: what is conventionally described as loss is simultaneously generation, as the target language provides new phonetic and rhythmic possibilities.
sentences.translation: 번역은 변증법적으로 재구성돼요: 전통적으로 상실로 기술되는 것이 동시에 생성이며, 목표 언어가 새로운 음성적, 리듬적 가능성을 제공해요.

sentences.text: The essay concludes that translation reveals meaning as emergent and perpetually renewable rather than fixed, positioning the translator as a creative agent rather than a neutral conduit.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 번역이 의미를 고정된 것이 아니라 창발적이고 영구적으로 갱신 가능한 것으로 드러낸다고 결론지으며, 번역자를 중립적 전달자가 아닌 창조적 행위자로 위치시켜요.

sentences.original: To translate a literary text is not merely to transpose words from one language into another but to negotiate an impossible set of competing demands — fidelity to the source text's meaning, attention to its rhythm and register, sensitivity to the target language's own expressive resources — each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others.
sentences.translation: 문학 텍스트를 번역하는 것은 단순히 한 언어에서 다른 언어로 단어를 옮기는 것이 아니라 불가능한 일련의 경쟁적 요구를 — 원문의 의미에 대한 충실함, 그것의 리듬과 어조에 대한 주의, 목표 언어 고유의 표현 자원에 대한 민감성을 — 협상하는 것이며, 이들 각각은 존중될 때 필연적으로 다른 것들을 타협시킨다.

grammarElements.text: To translate a literary text
grammarElements.label: 주어 (to 부정사구)

grammarElements.text: is not merely to transpose words from one language into another
grammarElements.label: 부정 보어 (not merely to)

grammarElements.text: but to negotiate an impossible set of competing demands
grammarElements.label: 대조 보어 (but to)

grammarElements.text: fidelity to the source text's meaning, attention to its rhythm and register, sensitivity to the target language's own expressive resources
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 삼중 동격구

grammarElements.text: each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (삽입 부사절)

vocabulary.example: Translation is more than transposing words.
The translator must balance fidelity to meaning with attention to rhythm.
Attention to the text's register is crucial in translation.

expressions.phrase: not merely to X but to Y
expressions.meaning: 단순히 X하는 것이 아니라 Y하는 것
expressions.usage: "not merely A but B"로 번역의 복잡성을 강조하는 대조적 정의 구조

expressions.phrase: an impossible set of competing demands
expressions.usage: 상호 양립 불가능한 요구들의 동시적 존재를 기술하는 표현

patterns.pattern: To [do X] is not merely to [simple version] but to negotiate [an impossible set of competing demands] — [A, B, C] — each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others
patterns.explanation: "not merely A but B" 구조로 행위를 재정의하고, 대시 삽입구로 경쟁적 요구를 열거한 뒤, 관계절로 그 요구들의 상호 양립 불가능성을 선언하는 패턴.
examples: To teach well is not merely to deliver information but to negotiate an impossible set of competing demands — clarity of explanation, depth of content, sensitivity to each student's level — each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others.

To govern democratically is not merely to implement majority decisions but to negotiate an impossible set of competing demands — efficiency of action, protection of minorities, transparency of process — each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others.

drillQuestions.question: "Transpose" means to _____ from one form to another.
"Fidelity" means _____ to the original.
"Register" in linguistics refers to the _____ of language use.
The competing demands "necessarily compromise" each other, meaning they _____.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 논제를 도입하는 첫 문장. "not merely to transpose but to negotiate"가 번역을 기계적 전환에서 복잡한 협상으로 재정의하며, 삼중 대시 삽입구(fidelity, attention, sensitivity)가 경쟁적 요구를 열거한다. 마무리 관계절이 이 요구들의 상호 양립 불가능성을 선언한다.

koreanAlignment.en: To translate a literary text is not merely to transpose words from one language into another
koreanAlignment.ko: 문학 텍스트를 번역하는 것은 단순히 한 언어에서 다른 언어로 단어를 옮기는 것이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but to negotiate an impossible set of competing demands
koreanAlignment.ko: 불가능한 일련의 경쟁적 요구를 협상하는 것이며

koreanAlignment.en: fidelity to the source text's meaning, attention to its rhythm and register, sensitivity to the target language's own expressive resources
koreanAlignment.ko: 원문의 의미에 대한 충실함, 그것의 리듬과 어조에 대한 주의, 목표 언어 고유의 표현 자원에 대한 민감성

koreanAlignment.en: each of which, when honored, necessarily compromises the others
koreanAlignment.ko: 이들 각각은 존중될 때 필연적으로 다른 것들을 타협시킨다

pronunciationNotes: transpose: /trænˈspoʊz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "트랜스포즈"에 가깝다.
fidelity: /fɪˈdelɪti/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "피델리티"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 경쟁적 요구의 열거 (enumeration of competing demands)

sentences.original: The translator confronts, at every sentence, the foundational problem that languages do not map onto one another with anything approaching one-to-one correspondence: a single word in the source language may carry connotations, etymological echoes, and cultural associations for which the target language offers no ready equivalent, forcing the translator to choose which dimension of meaning to preserve and which to sacrifice.
sentences.translation: 번역자는 매 문장에서 언어들이 일대일 대응에 조금도 가깝게 상호 대응하지 않는다는 근본적 문제에 직면한다: 원어의 단일 단어가 함축, 어원적 반향, 문화적 연상을 지닐 수 있으며 이에 대해 목표 언어는 손쉬운 등가물을 제공하지 않아, 번역자가 의미의 어떤 차원을 보존하고 어떤 차원을 희생할지 선택하도록 강제한다.

grammarElements.text: The translator confronts, at every sentence,
grammarElements.label: 동사 (삽입 부사구)

grammarElements.text: the foundational problem that languages do not map onto one another with anything approaching one-to-one correspondence
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (that 동격절)

grammarElements.text: a single word in the source language may carry connotations, etymological echoes, and cultural associations
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절

grammarElements.text: for which the target language offers no ready equivalent
grammarElements.label: 관계절

grammarElements.text: forcing the translator to choose which dimension of meaning to preserve and which to sacrifice
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (which 간접의문문 병렬)

vocabulary.example: A word may carry etymological echoes lost in translation.
Words carry connotations that differ across languages.
The target language offers no ready equivalent.

expressions.phrase: at every sentence
expressions.usage: 번역 작업의 끊임없는 난관을 강조하는 삽입 부사구

expressions.phrase: which dimension of meaning to preserve and which to sacrifice
expressions.meaning: 의미의 어떤 차원을 보존하고 어떤 것을 희생할지
expressions.usage: 번역의 불가피한 선택을 병렬 간접의문문으로 기술하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Agent] confronts, at every [unit], the foundational problem that [X does not map onto Y]: a single [element] may carry [A, B, and C] for which [Y offers no equivalent], forcing [agent] to choose which [dimension] to preserve and which to sacrifice
patterns.explanation: 삽입 부사구로 빈도를 강조하고, 콜론 뒤 구체적 사례로 문제를 예시하며, 분사구문으로 불가피한 선택을 기술하는 패턴.
examples: The historian confronts, at every source, the foundational problem that past minds do not map onto present categories: a single concept in the historical record may carry assumptions, institutional contexts, and social meanings for which contemporary vocabulary offers no ready equivalent, forcing the historian to choose which dimension of significance to reconstruct and which to acknowledge as irrecoverable.

drillQuestions.question: "Etymological" relates to the _____ of words.
"Connotations" are _____ meanings of a word.
"No ready equivalent" means there is no _____ match.
The translator must choose between preservation and _____.

sentences.notes: "at every sentence"의 삽입이 번역 작업의 끊임없는 난관을 강조한다. "etymological echoes"와 "cultural associations"가 단어의 다층적 의미를 구체화하며, "which to preserve and which to sacrifice"의 병렬이 번역의 불가피한 선택을 극적으로 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The translator confronts, at every sentence, the foundational problem
koreanAlignment.ko: 번역자는 매 문장에서 근본적 문제에 직면한다

koreanAlignment.en: that languages do not map onto one another with anything approaching one-to-one correspondence
koreanAlignment.ko: 언어들이 일대일 대응에 가깝게 상호 대응하지 않는다는

koreanAlignment.en: a single word in the source language may carry connotations, etymological echoes, and cultural associations
koreanAlignment.ko: 원어의 단일 단어가 함축, 어원적 반향, 문화적 연상을 지닐 수 있으며

koreanAlignment.en: for which the target language offers no ready equivalent
koreanAlignment.ko: 이에 대해 목표 언어는 손쉬운 등가물을 제공하지 않아

koreanAlignment.en: forcing the translator to choose which dimension of meaning to preserve and which to sacrifice
koreanAlignment.ko: 번역자가 의미의 어떤 차원을 보존하고 어떤 차원을 희생할지 선택하도록 강제한다

pronunciationNotes: etymological: /ˌetɪməˈlɑːdʒɪkəl/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "에티몰라지컬"에 가깝다.
connotations: /ˌkɑːnəˈteɪʃənz/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "카너테이션즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This is why the debate between domestication and foreignization — whether the translation should read as though the author had originally written in the target language, or whether it should retain the strangeness of the source — is not a technical dispute but a philosophical one, reflecting fundamentally different assumptions about what a translation is for.
sentences.translation: 이것이 바로 자국화와 이국화 사이의 논쟁 — 번역이 마치 저자가 원래 목표 언어로 쓴 것처럼 읽혀야 하는지, 아니면 원문의 낯섦을 유지해야 하는지 — 이 기술적 분쟁이 아니라 철학적 분쟁인 이유이며, 번역이 무엇을 위한 것인지에 대한 근본적으로 다른 가정을 반영한다.

grammarElements.text: This is why
grammarElements.label: 주절 (this is why)

grammarElements.text: the debate between domestication and foreignization
grammarElements.label: why절의 주어

grammarElements.text: whether the translation should read as though the author had originally written in the target language, or whether it should retain the strangeness of the source
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 whether 이중 절

grammarElements.text: is not a technical dispute but a philosophical one
grammarElements.label: 보어 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: reflecting fundamentally different assumptions about what a translation is for
grammarElements.label: 분사구문

vocabulary.meaning: 자국화 (번역에서 목표 문화에 맞게 변형하는 전략)
vocabulary.example: Domestication makes the text feel native to the reader.
Foreignization retains the strangeness of the source.
The foreignizing translator preserves the strangeness of the source.

expressions.phrase: This is why
expressions.usage: 앞선 논의에서 결론을 도출하는 연결 표현

expressions.phrase: not a technical dispute but a philosophical one
expressions.usage: 논쟁의 차원을 기술에서 철학으로 격상시키는 대조적 표현

patterns.pattern: This is why the debate between [A and B] — whether [option 1], or whether [option 2] — is not a [technical] dispute but a [philosophical] one, reflecting fundamentally different assumptions about [what X is for]
patterns.explanation: "This is why"로 이전 논의를 결론으로 연결하고, 대시 삽입구의 이중 "whether"절로 선택지를 제시하며, "not A but B"로 논쟁의 차원을 격상시키는 패턴.
examples: This is why the debate between formalism and contextualism — whether a poem should be read as an autonomous verbal structure, or whether it should be situated within its historical moment — is not a methodological dispute but a philosophical one, reflecting fundamentally different assumptions about what literature is for.

drillQuestions.question: "Domestication" in translation means making the text feel _____.
"Foreignization" preserves the _____ of the original.
The debate is described as _____ rather than technical.
"As though the author had originally written" uses _____ mood.

sentences.notes: 번역학의 핵심 이항대립(domestication vs. foreignization)을 도입하며, 이를 기술적 문제에서 철학적 문제로 격상시킨다. 이중 "whether"절이 두 전략의 핵심 차이를 명확히 기술하고, 마무리 분사구문이 이 논쟁의 존재론적 차원을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: This is why the debate between domestication and foreignization
koreanAlignment.ko: 이것이 바로 자국화와 이국화 사이의 논쟁이

koreanAlignment.en: whether the translation should read as though the author had originally written in the target language
koreanAlignment.ko: 번역이 마치 저자가 원래 목표 언어로 쓴 것처럼 읽혀야 하는지

koreanAlignment.en: or whether it should retain the strangeness of the source
koreanAlignment.ko: 아니면 원문의 낯섦을 유지해야 하는지

koreanAlignment.en: is not a technical dispute but a philosophical one
koreanAlignment.ko: 기술적 분쟁이 아니라 철학적 분쟁인 이유이며

koreanAlignment.en: reflecting fundamentally different assumptions about what a translation is for
koreanAlignment.ko: 번역이 무엇을 위한 것인지에 대한 근본적으로 다른 가정을 반영한다

pronunciationNotes: domestication: /dəˌmestɪˈkeɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "도메스티케이션"에 가깝다.
foreignization: /ˌfɔːrənɪˈzeɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "포러니제이션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The domesticating translator privileges accessibility, smoothing away the linguistic and cultural peculiarities of the original so that the reader encounters a text that feels native; the foreignizing translator, by contrast, insists on preserving those very peculiarities, accepting that the reader will sometimes stumble over unfamiliar constructions, because it is precisely in that stumbling that the reader encounters the otherness of the source culture.
sentences.translation: 자국화 번역자는 접근성을 특권화하여 원문의 언어적, 문화적 특이성을 매끄럽게 만들어 독자가 모국어처럼 느껴지는 텍스트를 만나게 한다; 이에 반해 이국화 번역자는 바로 그 특이성을 보존할 것을 주장하며, 독자가 때때로 낯선 구문에 걸려 넘어질 것을 수용하는데, 독자가 원천 문화의 타자성을 만나는 것은 정확히 그 걸려 넘어짐 속에서이기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The domesticating translator privileges accessibility
grammarElements.label: 주절 1

grammarElements.text: smoothing away the linguistic and cultural peculiarities of the original
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 1

grammarElements.text: so that the reader encounters a text that feels native
grammarElements.label: 목적절

grammarElements.text: the foreignizing translator, by contrast, insists on preserving those very peculiarities
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 주절 2 (대비)

grammarElements.text: accepting that the reader will sometimes stumble over unfamiliar constructions
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 2

grammarElements.text: because it is precisely in that stumbling that the reader encounters the otherness of the source culture
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (분열문)

vocabulary.example: The domesticating translator smooths away the peculiarities of the original.
The reader encounters the otherness of the source culture.
The domesticating translator privileges accessibility.

expressions.phrase: smoothing away the peculiarities
expressions.usage: 자국화 번역의 핵심 전략을 촉각적 비유로 표현

expressions.phrase: it is precisely in that stumbling that
expressions.meaning: 정확히 그 걸려 넘어짐 속에서 ~하는 것이다
expressions.usage: 분열문으로 역설적 가치를 강조하는 구조

patterns.pattern: The [A-ist] privileges [X], [smoothing away peculiarities] so that [result]; the [B-ist], by contrast, insists on [preserving] those very [peculiarities], accepting [cost], because it is precisely in [that cost] that [value] is encountered
patterns.explanation: 세미콜론으로 두 전략을 대비하고, 이국화 전략의 역설적 가치를 분열문으로 강조하는 패턴.
examples: The mainstream publisher privileges marketability, smoothing away the experimental features of the manuscript so that the reader encounters a familiar narrative; the avant-garde publisher, by contrast, insists on preserving those very features, accepting that the reader will sometimes struggle with unconventional form, because it is precisely in that struggle that the reader encounters the newness of the artistic vision.

drillQuestions.question: "Peculiarities" are _____ characteristics.
"Otherness" refers to the quality of being _____.
The domesticating translator prioritizes _____.
The foreignizing translator sees value in the reader's _____.

sentences.notes: 세미콜론 대비 구조가 두 번역 전략을 체계적으로 비교한다. "smoothing away"의 촉각적 비유와 "stumbling"의 신체적 비유가 번역의 두 극단을 생생하게 전달하며, 마무리 분열문이 이국화 전략의 역설적 가치 — 걸림이 곧 만남 — 를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The domesticating translator privileges accessibility
koreanAlignment.ko: 자국화 번역자는 접근성을 특권화하여

koreanAlignment.en: smoothing away the linguistic and cultural peculiarities of the original
koreanAlignment.ko: 원문의 언어적, 문화적 특이성을 매끄럽게 만들어

koreanAlignment.en: so that the reader encounters a text that feels native
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자가 모국어처럼 느껴지는 텍스트를 만나게 한다

koreanAlignment.en: the foreignizing translator, by contrast, insists on preserving those very peculiarities
koreanAlignment.ko: 이에 반해 이국화 번역자는 바로 그 특이성을 보존할 것을 주장하며

koreanAlignment.en: because it is precisely in that stumbling that the reader encounters the otherness of the source culture
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자가 원천 문화의 타자성을 만나는 것은 정확히 그 걸려 넘어짐 속에서이기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: peculiarities: /pɪˌkjuːliˈærɪtiz/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "피큘리에리티즈"에 가깝다.
otherness: /ˈʌðərnəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "아더니스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: What is lost in even the finest translation is not merely a list of untranslatable words but an entire ecology of sound and sense: the phonetic texture of the original prose, the rhythmic patterns that govern the reader's breath, the syntactic structures that distribute emphasis in ways the target language cannot replicate without distortion.
sentences.translation: 가장 훌륭한 번역에서조차 상실되는 것은 단순히 번역 불가능한 단어들의 목록이 아니라 소리와 의미의 전체 생태계이다: 원문 산문의 음성적 질감, 독자의 호흡을 지배하는 리듬적 패턴, 목표 언어가 왜곡 없이 복제할 수 없는 방식으로 강조를 배분하는 구문적 구조.

grammarElements.text: What is lost in even the finest translation
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절)

grammarElements.text: is not merely a list of untranslatable words
grammarElements.label: 부정 보어

grammarElements.text: but an entire ecology of sound and sense
grammarElements.label: 대조 보어 (but)

grammarElements.text: the phonetic texture of the original prose
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 구체화 1

grammarElements.text: the rhythmic patterns that govern the reader's breath
grammarElements.label: 구체화 2 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: the syntactic structures that distribute emphasis in ways the target language cannot replicate without distortion
grammarElements.label: 구체화 3 (관계절 중첩)

vocabulary.example: Translation loses an entire ecology of sound and sense.
The phonetic texture of the prose is lost in translation.
Some words are considered fundamentally untranslatable.

expressions.phrase: an entire ecology of sound and sense
expressions.usage: 상실을 개별 항목이 아닌 유기적 체계로 개념화하는 생태학적 비유

expressions.phrase: the rhythmic patterns that govern the reader's breath
expressions.usage: 산문의 리듬이 독자의 신체에 미치는 영향을 기술하는 표현

patterns.pattern: What is lost in even [the finest X] is not merely [a list of items] but [an entire ecology of Y]: [specific 1], [specific 2], [specific 3 that cannot be replicated without Z]
patterns.explanation: "What is lost" 주어절로 상실을 주제화하고, "not merely A but B" 대조로 상실의 규모를 확장하며, 콜론 뒤 삼중 구체화로 생태계를 기술하는 패턴.
examples: What is lost when a building is demolished is not merely a list of architectural features but an entire ecology of space and memory: the quality of light in the hallways, the acoustic properties that shaped conversations, the spatial relationships that organized human movement in ways no blueprint can reconstruct.

drillQuestions.question: "Ecology" here is used as a _____ for a system of interrelated elements.
"Phonetic texture" refers to the _____ quality of language.
"Untranslatable" means it _____ be translated.
The "reader's breath" is governed by rhythmic _____.

sentences.notes: "ecology"의 생태학적 비유가 번역의 상실을 개별 항목이 아닌 유기적 체계의 파괴로 개념화한다. "the rhythmic patterns that govern the reader's breath"가 산문의 리듬과 신체의 관계를 시적으로 표현하며, 삼중 구체화(phonetic, rhythmic, syntactic)가 상실의 다층적 차원을 체계적으로 열거한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What is lost in even the finest translation
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 훌륭한 번역에서조차 상실되는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: is not merely a list of untranslatable words but an entire ecology of sound and sense
koreanAlignment.ko: 단순히 번역 불가능한 단어들의 목록이 아니라 소리와 의미의 전체 생태계이다

koreanAlignment.en: the phonetic texture of the original prose
koreanAlignment.ko: 원문 산문의 음성적 질감

koreanAlignment.en: the rhythmic patterns that govern the reader's breath
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자의 호흡을 지배하는 리듬적 패턴

koreanAlignment.en: the syntactic structures that distribute emphasis in ways the target language cannot replicate without distortion
koreanAlignment.ko: 목표 언어가 왜곡 없이 복제할 수 없는 방식으로 강조를 배분하는 구문적 구조

pronunciationNotes: ecology: /iˈkɑːlədʒi/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이칼러지"에 가깝다.
phonetic: /fəˈnetɪk/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼네틱"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Consider the challenge of translating a novel whose meaning depends partly on register shifts — passages where the narrator's language moves between formal and colloquial, literary and vernacular — and recognize that in many target languages, the register system is organized along entirely different social and grammatical axes, so that what reads as a subtle tonal shift in the original may become either imperceptible or grotesquely exaggerated in translation.
sentences.translation: 의미가 부분적으로 어조 전환에 의존하는 소설을 번역하는 도전을 생각해 보라 — 화자의 언어가 격식적 것과 구어적 것 사이를, 문어적 것과 구어적 것 사이를 이동하는 대목들 — 그리고 많은 목표 언어에서 어조 체계가 전적으로 다른 사회적, 문법적 축을 따라 조직되어 있어서, 원문에서 미묘한 어조적 전환으로 읽히는 것이 번역에서는 감지 불가능하거나 기괴하게 과장될 수 있다는 것을 인식하라.

grammarElements.text: Consider the challenge of translating a novel whose meaning depends partly on register shifts
grammarElements.label: 명령문 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: passages where the narrator's language moves between formal and colloquial, literary and vernacular
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (where절)

grammarElements.text: and recognize that in many target languages
grammarElements.label: 병렬 명령문

grammarElements.text: the register system is organized along entirely different social and grammatical axes
grammarElements.label: that 명사절

grammarElements.text: so that what reads as a subtle tonal shift in the original may become either imperceptible or grotesquely exaggerated in translation
grammarElements.label: 결과절 (either A or B)

vocabulary.example: The narrator shifts between literary and vernacular register.
The tonal shift may become grotesquely exaggerated.
The shift may become imperceptible in translation.

expressions.phrase: organized along entirely different axes
expressions.usage: 체계의 구조적 비교불가능성을 기하학적 비유로 표현하는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: either imperceptible or grotesquely exaggerated
expressions.usage: 극단적 양분법으로 번역의 딜레마를 기술하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Consider the challenge of [translating X whose meaning depends on Y] — [specification] — and recognize that [in many contexts], [the system is organized differently], so that what [reads as subtle] may become either [invisible] or [exaggerated]
patterns.explanation: "Consider" 명령문으로 구체적 도전을 제시하고, "and recognize" 병렬로 구조적 비교불가능성을 밝히며, "either A or B"로 번역의 양극적 위험을 기술하는 패턴.
examples: Consider the challenge of performing a play whose humor depends on wordplay — passages where the dialogue exploits homonyms and double meanings — and recognize that in many other languages, the phonetic system is organized along entirely different principles, so that what works as a subtle comic double entendre in the original may become either incomprehensible or laboriously explained in performance.

drillQuestions.question: "Register shifts" are changes in the _____ of language.
"Vernacular" refers to _____ everyday language.
"Grotesquely" means in a _____ distorted way.
The "axes" metaphor comes from _____.

sentences.notes: 어조 전환(register shift)이라는 구체적 번역 문제를 사례로 들어 체계적 비교불가능성을 입증한다. "organized along entirely different axes"의 기하학적 비유가 어조 체계의 구조적 차이를 효과적으로 표현하며, "either imperceptible or grotesquely exaggerated"의 양극적 대비가 번역의 딜레마를 극화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Consider the challenge of translating a novel whose meaning depends partly on register shifts
koreanAlignment.ko: 의미가 부분적으로 어조 전환에 의존하는 소설을 번역하는 도전을 생각해 보라

koreanAlignment.en: passages where the narrator's language moves between formal and colloquial, literary and vernacular
koreanAlignment.ko: 화자의 언어가 격식적 것과 구어적 것 사이를 이동하는 대목들

koreanAlignment.en: and recognize that in many target languages, the register system is organized along entirely different social and grammatical axes
koreanAlignment.ko: 많은 목표 언어에서 어조 체계가 전적으로 다른 사회적, 문법적 축을 따라 조직되어 있다는 것을 인식하라

koreanAlignment.en: so that what reads as a subtle tonal shift in the original
koreanAlignment.ko: 원문에서 미묘한 어조적 전환으로 읽히는 것이

koreanAlignment.en: may become either imperceptible or grotesquely exaggerated in translation
koreanAlignment.ko: 번역에서는 감지 불가능하거나 기괴하게 과장될 수 있다

pronunciationNotes: vernacular: /vərˈnækjʊlər/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "버내큘러"에 가깝다.
grotesquely: /ɡroʊˈteskli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "그로테스클리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Yet to describe translation as loss is to tell only half the story, for every competent translation also creates something the original lacks: it provides a new phonetic body for the text, generates fresh rhythmic possibilities unavailable in the source language, and occasionally discovers resonances and ambiguities that the original, constrained by its own linguistic system, could not have produced.
sentences.translation: 그러나 번역을 상실로 기술하는 것은 이야기의 반만 말하는 것인데, 모든 유능한 번역은 원문에 없는 무언가를 또한 창조하기 때문이다: 텍스트에 새로운 음성적 몸체를 제공하고, 원어에서 이용 불가능한 신선한 리듬적 가능성을 생성하며, 때로는 원문이 자체 언어 체계에 의해 제약되어 생산할 수 없었던 공명과 모호성을 발견한다.

grammarElements.text: Yet to describe translation as loss
grammarElements.label: 주어 (Yet + to 부정사구)

grammarElements.text: is to tell only half the story
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: for every competent translation also creates something the original lacks
grammarElements.label: for 이유절

grammarElements.text: it provides a new phonetic body for the text
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명 1

grammarElements.text: generates fresh rhythmic possibilities unavailable in the source language
grammarElements.label: 설명 2

grammarElements.text: and occasionally discovers resonances and ambiguities that the original, constrained by its own linguistic system, could not have produced
grammarElements.label: 설명 3 (관계절 + 분사 삽입구)

vocabulary.example: Translation may discover new resonances in the text.
The original is constrained by its own linguistic system.
Translation gives the text a new phonetic body.

expressions.phrase: to tell only half the story
expressions.usage: 불완전한 설명을 지적하며 보완적 관점을 도입하는 관용적 표현

expressions.phrase: a new phonetic body for the text
expressions.usage: 텍스트의 물질적 구현을 신체적 비유로 표현하는 문학 비평적 표현

patterns.pattern: Yet to describe [X] as [loss] is to tell only half the story, for every [competent X] also creates [something]: it [A], [B], and occasionally [C that the original could not have produced]
patterns.explanation: "Yet"으로 변증법적 전환을 수행하고, "to tell only half the story"로 기존 관점의 불완전성을 지적하며, 콜론 뒤 삼중 절로 창조적 차원을 열거하는 패턴.
examples: Yet to describe aging as decline is to tell only half the story, for every decade of experience also creates something youth lacks: it provides a depth of perspective unavailable to the young, generates new forms of patience and resilience, and occasionally discovers wisdom that earlier stages of life, constrained by their own urgencies, could not have produced.

drillQuestions.question: "To tell only half the story" means the account is _____.
A "phonetic body" for a text means its new _____ form.
"Resonances" are meanings that _____ across contexts.
The "Yet" at the beginning signals a _____ turn.

sentences.notes: "Yet"이 에세이의 변증법적 전환을 수행한다: 상실에서 창조로. "a new phonetic body"의 신체적 비유가 텍스트의 물질적 재구현을 생생하게 표현하며, "constrained by its own linguistic system"의 삽입 분사구문이 원문의 한계를 밝혀 번역의 창조적 가능성을 역설적으로 입증한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet to describe translation as loss is to tell only half the story
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 번역을 상실로 기술하는 것은 이야기의 반만 말하는 것인데

koreanAlignment.en: for every competent translation also creates something the original lacks
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 유능한 번역은 원문에 없는 무언가를 또한 창조하기 때문이다

koreanAlignment.en: it provides a new phonetic body for the text
koreanAlignment.ko: 텍스트에 새로운 음성적 몸체를 제공하고

koreanAlignment.en: generates fresh rhythmic possibilities unavailable in the source language
koreanAlignment.ko: 원어에서 이용 불가능한 신선한 리듬적 가능성을 생성하며

koreanAlignment.en: and occasionally discovers resonances and ambiguities that the original could not have produced
koreanAlignment.ko: 때로는 원문이 생산할 수 없었던 공명과 모호성을 발견한다

pronunciationNotes: resonances: /ˈrezənənsɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레저넌시즈"에 가깝다.
constrained: /kənˈstreɪnd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨스트레인드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The notion of literary fidelity, therefore, is far more complex than simple accuracy of denotation; it encompasses a translator's intuition about which qualities of the source text are most essential — its tone, its pace, its emotional architecture — and a willingness to depart from literal meaning when literal meaning would betray the spirit of the whole.
sentences.translation: 따라서 문학적 충실함의 개념은 단순한 지시적 의미의 정확성보다 훨씬 복잡하다; 이는 원문의 어떤 특성이 가장 본질적인지에 대한 번역자의 직관을 — 그것의 어조, 속도, 정서적 구조를 — 포괄하며, 축자적 의미가 전체의 정신을 배반할 때 축자적 의미에서 벗어나는 의지를 포함한다.

grammarElements.text: The notion of literary fidelity, therefore,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 접속부사)

grammarElements.text: is far more complex than simple accuracy of denotation
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 비교급

grammarElements.text: it encompasses a translator's intuition about which qualities of the source text are most essential
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 설명절

grammarElements.text: its tone, its pace, its emotional architecture
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 삼중 동격구

grammarElements.text: and a willingness to depart from literal meaning when literal meaning would betray the spirit of the whole
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 (when 조건절)

vocabulary.example: Fidelity goes beyond simple accuracy of denotation.
The translator must preserve the text's emotional architecture.
Literal translation can betray the spirit of the original.

expressions.phrase: far more complex than
expressions.usage: 기존의 단순한 이해를 넘어서는 복잡성을 주장하는 비교 표현

expressions.phrase: depart from literal meaning when literal meaning would betray
expressions.meaning: 축자적 의미가 배반할 때 축자적 의미에서 벗어나다
expressions.usage: "literal meaning"의 의도적 반복으로 역설을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The notion of [fidelity], therefore, is far more complex than [simple accuracy]; it encompasses [intuition about X] — [A, B, C] — and a willingness to [depart from Y] when [Y would betray Z]
patterns.explanation: 핵심 개념을 재정의하고, 대시 삽입구로 구체화하며, "depart from ... when ... would betray"로 역설적 충실함의 원칙을 밝히는 패턴.
examples: The notion of historical accuracy, therefore, is far more complex than simple factual correctness; it encompasses a historian's judgment about which aspects of the past are most significant — its structures, its mentalities, its felt experience — and a willingness to depart from strict chronology when strict chronology would betray the lived texture of the era.

drillQuestions.question: "Denotation" is the _____ meaning of a word.
"Emotional architecture" refers to the _____ structure of feelings in a text.
A translator may need to "depart from" literal meaning to be truly _____.
"Betray the spirit" means to violate the _____ essence.

sentences.notes: "literary fidelity"를 단순한 정확성에서 복합적 직관으로 재정의하는 핵심 문장. "its tone, its pace, its emotional architecture"의 삼중 삽입구가 충실함의 다층적 대상을 명시하며, "depart from literal meaning when literal meaning would betray"의 역설이 번역의 근본적 아이러니를 포착한다: 때로는 불충실해야 충실하다.

koreanAlignment.en: The notion of literary fidelity, therefore, is far more complex than simple accuracy of denotation
koreanAlignment.ko: 따라서 문학적 충실함의 개념은 단순한 지시적 의미의 정확성보다 훨씬 복잡하다

koreanAlignment.en: it encompasses a translator's intuition about which qualities of the source text are most essential
koreanAlignment.ko: 이는 원문의 어떤 특성이 가장 본질적인지에 대한 번역자의 직관을 포괄하며

koreanAlignment.en: its tone, its pace, its emotional architecture
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것의 어조, 속도, 정서적 구조

koreanAlignment.en: and a willingness to depart from literal meaning
koreanAlignment.ko: 축자적 의미에서 벗어나는 의지를 포함한다

koreanAlignment.en: when literal meaning would betray the spirit of the whole
koreanAlignment.ko: 축자적 의미가 전체의 정신을 배반할 때

pronunciationNotes: denotation: /ˌdiːnoʊˈteɪʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "디노테이션"에 가깝다.
architecture: /ˈɑːrkɪtektʃər/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "아키텍처"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The best translations, like the best critical interpretations, do not pretend to be transparent windows onto the original but acknowledge themselves as readings — partial, situated, and inevitably shaped by the translator's own literary sensibility, cultural position, and historical moment.
sentences.translation: 최고의 번역은, 최고의 비평적 해석처럼, 원문으로의 투명한 창문인 척하지 않고 자신을 읽기로 인정한다 — 부분적이고, 상황적이며, 번역자 자신의 문학적 감수성, 문화적 위치, 역사적 순간에 의해 불가피하게 형성된.

grammarElements.text: The best translations, like the best critical interpretations,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 비교구)

grammarElements.text: do not pretend to be transparent windows onto the original
grammarElements.label: 동사 (부정)

grammarElements.text: but acknowledge themselves as readings
grammarElements.label: 대조 동사 (but)

grammarElements.text: partial, situated, and inevitably shaped by the translator's own literary sensibility, cultural position, and historical moment
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 삼중 형용사구 (분사 포함)

vocabulary.example: The translation reflects the translator's literary sensibility.
Every translation is situated in a particular cultural context.
Translations are not transparent windows onto the original.

expressions.phrase: transparent windows onto the original
expressions.usage: 번역의 이상화된 비유를 거부하기 위해 도입하는 시각적 비유

expressions.phrase: acknowledge themselves as readings
expressions.usage: 번역의 해석적 성격을 자기인식적으로 표현하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: The best [translations], like the best [interpretations], do not pretend to be [transparent medium] but acknowledge themselves as [readings] — [partial, situated, and inevitably shaped by X, Y, and Z]
patterns.explanation: 이상화된 투명성을 거부하고, 자기인식적 해석으로 재정의하며, 대시 뒤 삼중 구체화로 해석의 조건성을 명시하는 패턴.
examples: The best histories, like the best biographies, do not pretend to be objective mirrors of the past but acknowledge themselves as constructions — selective, interpretive, and inevitably shaped by the historian's own theoretical commitments, political context, and archival limitations.

drillQuestions.question: "Transparent windows" implies translations should be _____, which the author rejects.
"Situated" means located within a specific _____.
Translations are described as "readings," meaning they are _____.
The translator's "sensibility" is their aesthetic _____.

sentences.notes: "transparent windows"의 시각적 비유가 번역의 이상화된 투명성을 비판적으로 거부한다. "acknowledge themselves as readings"가 번역을 해석적 행위로 재정의하며, 대시 뒤 삼중 조건(partial, situated, shaped)이 모든 번역의 상황적 조건성을 체계적으로 명시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The best translations, like the best critical interpretations
koreanAlignment.ko: 최고의 번역은 최고의 비평적 해석처럼

koreanAlignment.en: do not pretend to be transparent windows onto the original
koreanAlignment.ko: 원문으로의 투명한 창문인 척하지 않고

koreanAlignment.en: but acknowledge themselves as readings
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신을 읽기로 인정한다

koreanAlignment.en: partial, situated, and inevitably shaped by the translator's own literary sensibility, cultural position, and historical moment
koreanAlignment.ko: 부분적이고, 상황적이며, 번역자 자신의 문학적 감수성, 문화적 위치, 역사적 순간에 의해 불가피하게 형성된

pronunciationNotes: sensibility: /ˌsensəˈbɪlɪti/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "센서빌리티"에 가깝다.
situated: /ˈsɪtʃueɪtɪd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "시츄에이티드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: What translation finally reveals is not the inadequacy of language but its inexhaustible generativity: every text, re-entered through a new linguistic system, yields possibilities that its author could not have foreseen, reminding us that meaning is not a fixed property of words but an emergent phenomenon, perpetually renewed by every act of reading across linguistic and cultural borders.
sentences.translation: 번역이 궁극적으로 드러내는 것은 언어의 부적절함이 아니라 그것의 무궁무진한 생성력이다: 모든 텍스트는 새로운 언어 체계를 통해 재진입될 때, 저자가 예견할 수 없었던 가능성을 산출하며, 의미가 단어의 고정된 속성이 아니라 언어적, 문화적 경계를 가로지르는 모든 읽기 행위에 의해 영구적으로 갱신되는 창발적 현상이라는 것을 상기시킨다.

grammarElements.text: What translation finally reveals
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절)

grammarElements.text: is not the inadequacy of language but its inexhaustible generativity
grammarElements.label: 보어 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: every text, re-entered through a new linguistic system, yields possibilities that its author could not have foreseen
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (삽입 분사구문 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: reminding us that meaning is not a fixed property of words but an emergent phenomenon
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: perpetually renewed by every act of reading across linguistic and cultural borders
grammarElements.label: 분사 수식구

vocabulary.example: Translation reveals language's inexhaustible generativity.
Meaning is an emergent phenomenon, not a fixed property.
Meaning is perpetually renewed by reading.

expressions.phrase: not the inadequacy of language but its inexhaustible generativity
expressions.meaning: 언어의 부적절함이 아니라 그것의 무궁무진한 생성력
expressions.usage: 비관적 관점을 낙관적 관점으로 전환하는 대조적 재정의

expressions.phrase: an emergent phenomenon, perpetually renewed
expressions.usage: 의미의 동적 성격을 복잡계 이론의 용어로 기술하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: What [X] finally reveals is not [pessimistic view] but [optimistic view]: every [instance], re-entered through [new system], yields [possibilities], reminding us that [meaning] is not [fixed] but [emergent], perpetually [renewed] by [every act of Y]
patterns.explanation: 에세이의 결론적 통찰을 "not A but B" 대조로 제시하고, 콜론 뒤 구체화와 분사구문으로 의미의 동적 성격을 선언하는 마무리 패턴.
examples: What the history of misreadings finally reveals is not the failure of interpretation but its inexhaustible productivity: every canonical text, re-entered through a new critical framework, yields insights its original audience could not have perceived, reminding us that significance is not a fixed deposit within the text but an emergent phenomenon, perpetually regenerated by every act of reading across temporal and ideological borders.

drillQuestions.question: "Generativity" means the capacity to _____ new things.
"Emergent" describes something that _____ from a process.
Translation reveals language's _____, not its inadequacy.
Meaning is "perpetually renewed," meaning it _____ stops changing.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 결론적 반전. "not the inadequacy but the generativity"가 번역에 대한 비관을 낙관으로 전환한다. "emergent phenomenon"이 복잡계 이론의 용어를 차용하여 의미의 동적 성격을 기술하며, "perpetually renewed by every act of reading"이 의미의 끊임없는 생성을 선언하는 낙관적 마무리를 형성한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What translation finally reveals is not the inadequacy of language but its inexhaustible generativity
koreanAlignment.ko: 번역이 궁극적으로 드러내는 것은 언어의 부적절함이 아니라 그것의 무궁무진한 생성력이다

koreanAlignment.en: every text, re-entered through a new linguistic system, yields possibilities that its author could not have foreseen
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 텍스트는 새로운 언어 체계를 통해 재진입될 때 저자가 예견할 수 없었던 가능성을 산출하며

koreanAlignment.en: reminding us that meaning is not a fixed property of words
koreanAlignment.ko: 의미가 단어의 고정된 속성이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but an emergent phenomenon, perpetually renewed
koreanAlignment.ko: 영구적으로 갱신되는 창발적 현상이라는 것을 상기시킨다

koreanAlignment.en: by every act of reading across linguistic and cultural borders
koreanAlignment.ko: 언어적, 문화적 경계를 가로지르는 모든 읽기 행위에 의해

pronunciationNotes: generativity: /ˌdʒenərəˈtɪvɪti/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "제너러티비티"에 가깝다.
emergent: /ɪˈmɜːrdʒənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이머전트"에 가깝다.
